title: Building a Resilient Currency API with .NET, Polly, and Redis
excerpt: A deep dive into building a production-ready currency conversion API with circuit breakers, retry policies, distributed caching, and OpenTelemetry all in ASP.NET Core.
body:

",l(s.p,{children:["Modern APIs can't afford to fail silently. When you're building a service that depends on a third-party provider like a currency rate API you need to think about ",i(s.strong,{children:"resilience from day one"}),"."]}),"
",i(s.p,{children:"In this post, I'll walk through the architecture decisions behind a production-ready currency converter API I recently built."}),"
",i(s.h2,{id:"the-problem",children:i(s.a,{href:"#the-problem",children:"The Problem"})}),"
",i(s.p,{children:"Third-party currency rate providers:"}),"
",l(s.ul,{children:["
",i(s.li,{children:"Have downtime"}),"
",i(s.li,{children:"Return stale data"}),"
",i(s.li,{children:"Rate-limit aggressively"}),"
"]}),"
",i(s.p,{children:"A naive implementation that calls the provider on every request will fail in production. We need something smarter."}),"
",i(s.h2,{id:"the-architecture",children:i(s.a,{href:"#the-architecture",children:"The Architecture"})}),"
",i(s.p,{children:"Here's the high-level flow:"}),"
",i(s.pre,{children:i(s.code,{children:"Client Request
     │
     ▼
Rate Limiter (IP-based)
     │
     ▼
Cache Check (Redis)
     │
     ├─── HIT ──▶ Return cached rate
     │
     └─── MISS ──▶ Polly Pipeline
                        │
                        ├── Retry (3x with exponential backoff)
                        ├── Circuit Breaker (5 failures → open 30s)
                        └── Timeout (5s per attempt)
"})}),"
",i(s.h2,{id:"implementing-the-cache-layer",children:i(s.a,{href:"#implementing-the-cache-layer",children:"Implementing the Cache Layer"})}),"
",i(s.p,{children:"Redis is our first line of defense. Rates don't change by the second a 5-minute TTL is entirely acceptable for most use cases."}),"
